content.problemStatement:
Om has an array AAA of size NNN and an integer BBB.

He wants to find whether there exists a **non-empty** subsequence of AAA such that the bitwise AND of all elements in the subsequence is equal to BBB.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and BBB — the size of array and the target integer, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, the ithi^{th}ith integer representing the element at the ithi^{th}ith index.




Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if any such subsequence exists and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example `YES`, `Yes`, `yes`, and `yES` are all considered the same.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 6⋅1056\cdot 10^{5}6⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the subsequence [A2,A6][A_2, A_6][A2​,A6​]. The bitwise AND of the elements is 7&13=57\& 13 = 57&13=5.
Test case 222: It can be shown that no subsequence satisfies the condition.
